prompt: Macro photography of a heavy, scarred wooden workbench. Scattered randomly across the wood is a chaotic, noisy arrangement of brass nails. A thin, flexible strip of pale wood (a boatbuilder's spline) has been forced to violently zigzag, buckle, and bend at extreme, unnatural angles by heavy cast-iron clamps so that its edge physically touches absolutely every single brass nail. The wood looks stressed and strained. Next to it, an elegant, smoothly sweeping curve is lightly etched into the wood, ignoring the scattered nails entirely. Cinematic workshop lighting, photorealistic, shallow depth of field, 8k resolution. --style raw --v 6.0
metaphor: A physical wooden spline violently clamped to intersect every randomly scattered brass nail represents an algorithm memorizing noisy training data, completely losing the ability to generalize.
